title: Euphorbia ridleyi
intro: Euphorbia ridleyi, also known as Ridley's Spurge, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Euphorbia ridleyi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional.

uses: Euphorbia ridleyi has been reported to have various medicinal properties, although its traditional uses are not well-documented.
